title: Tear Gas & CS Gas Decontamination
summary: Immediate steps for chemical irritant exposure.
content:
1. Do NOT rub your eyes or face — this embeds chemical crystals deeper into skin and eyes.
2. Flush Eyes: Flush eyes outwards with large amounts of clean saline solution or cold water for 10–15 minutes.
3. Air Flow: Move immediately to fresh high ground facing the wind.
4. Skin Decon: Wash exposed skin with cold water and mild liquid soap. Hot water opens skin pores and worsens burning.

title: Pepper Spray (OC Spray) Relief
content:
1. Remove Contact Lenses immediately with clean hands and discard them.
2. Flush continuously with sterile water or cold saline.
3. Do not apply oil, butter, or petroleum jelly — oils trap OC pepper resins against the skin.

title: Crush Injury & Crowd Compression Response
summary: Managing crowd pressure and compression asphyxia.
content:
1. Stance: Keep feet wide apart like a boxer, knees slightly bent, and hold hands up near your chest like a shield to preserve chest expansion space.
2. If Fallen: Roll into a ball, cover your head with arms, and turn onto your side to protect internal organs.
